title: Build a redundancy / "this already exists" guard (sprawl detection) as the next feature
context: From field commentary (YouTube: 'The most valuable dev skill in the AI era', @awesome-coding, UTKVB_tlyFw) whose central technical claim mirrors Hunch's thesis: LLMs optimize on LOCAL context windows, so they add factories/adapters/duplicate helpers and re-implement deps that already exist elsewhere — 'brilliant at micro-architecture, no management of the full picture.' Hunch uniquely HAS the full-picture graph (symbols/edges/components/fan-in), so it can catch this where a diff-only reviewer can't.
decision: Add a deterministic, ADVISORY redundancy dimension to the check/verdict pipeline: when a diff ADDS an exported symbol whose name already exists as an exported symbol in a DIFFERENT file (likely re-implementation), flag it with the existing location. Surface it in CheckReport (new `redundant` list), hunch check, the CI guard, and hunch_merge_verdict. Advisory-only — never blocks (heuristic, false-positive-prone), consistent with the near/blast-radius philosophy. Scope v1 to duplicate EXPORTED symbols with a stopword list (skip index/handler/default/get/set/run/main/test/etc.) and a length floor to cut noise; cap output. Reuses analyzeDiff(addedSymbols) + the symbols store + name matching — no new infra.
consequences: It is the COMPLEMENT to the existing guards: those catch breaking invariants / reversing decisions; this catches needless ADDITION/sprawl (the video's central failure mode).
Heuristic → must stay advisory and noise-controlled (stopwords, length floor, exported-only, cap); never enters the block path.

Two related ideas deferred to the roadmap: inline why-comment <-> constraint/decision capture, and a multi-candidate `hunch compare` verdict (rank N candidate diffs) matching the 'evaluate 5 solutions' seniority shift.
alternatives_rejected: Dep-duplication detection ('lodash duplicates internal utils') in v1 — needs semantic capability mapping, too fuzzy/noisy for a first cut; defer.
Making it a blocking gate — false positives on legitimately same-named symbols would erode trust; advisory only.
